name: Shelly Dimmer
description: The `shelly_dimmer` component adds support for the dimming and power-metering functionality that can be found the [Shelly Dimmer 2](https://shelly.cloud/knowledge-base/devices/shelly-dimmer-2/). The interaction with mains is done via an STM32 microcontroller that is automatically (when configured) flashed with an [open source firmware](https://github.com/jamesturton/shelly-dimmer-stm32). A detailed analysis of the Shelly Dimmer 2 hardware is given on [arendst/Tasmota#6914](https://github.com/arendst/Tasmota/issues/6914).

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name of the light. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the light in the frontend.

config_entries.label: UART ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID of the UART hub.

config_entries.label: Restore Mode
config_entries.description: Control how the light attempts to restore state on bootup.

options.label: Attempt to restore state and default to OFF if not possible to restore.
Attempt to restore state and default to ON.
Always initialize the light as ON on bootup.
Attempt to restore state inverted from the previous state and default to OFF.
Attempt to restore state inverted from the previous state and default to ON.
Attempt to restore state but initialize the light as OFF.
Attempt to restore state but initialize the light as ON.

config_entries.label: Boot0 Pin
config_entries.description: Pin connected with "BOOT0" of STM32. The default is "GPIO4".

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Current
config_entries.description: Sensor of the current in Amperes. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor).

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name for the sensor. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the sensor in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Device Class
config_entries.description: The device class for the sensor. See [https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/sensor/#device-class](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/sensor/#device-class) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default device class of a sensor.

config_entries.label: Unit Of Measurement
config_entries.description: Manually set the unit of measurement the sensor should advertise its values with. This does not actually do any maths (conversion between units).

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Accuracy Decimals
config_entries.description: Set the number of digits after the decimal point that data consumers should use. While this does not change the value sent across the API, Home Assistant's ESPHome integration will round the value accordingly before using or storing it. The number of digits will also be used to set the entity's default display precision. Thus, one can display fewer decimals, but cannot increase precision of sensor values in Home Assistant. (The default value varies depending on component and sensor.)

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Expire After
config_entries.description: Manually set the time in which the sensor values should be marked as “expired”/“unknown”. Not providing any value means no expiry.

config_entries.label: Filters
config_entries.description: Specify filters to use for some basic transforming of values. See [Sensor Filters](https://esphome.io/components/sensor#sensor-filters) for more information.

config_entries.label: Force Update
config_entries.description: If true, this option will force the frontend (usually Home Assistant) to create a state changed event when the sensor updates even if the value stayed the same. Some applications like Grafana require this when working with Home Assistant, but beware it can significantly increase the database size. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: State Class
config_entries.description: The state class for the sensor. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/sensor/#available-state-classes](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/sensor/#available-state-classes) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default state class of a sensor.

config_entries.label: Nrst Pin
config_entries.description: Pin connected with "NRST" of STM32. The default is "GPIO5".

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Power
config_entries.description: Sensor of the active power in Watts. Only accurate if neutral is connected. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor).

config_entries.description: Sensor of the voltage in Volts. Only accurate if neutral is connected. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor).

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Blue
config_entries.description: The blue channel of the light.

config_entries.label: Brightness
config_entries.description: The primary brightness of the light; applies to all channels (both color and white) of the light.

config_entries.label: Cold White
config_entries.description: The brightness of the cold white channel. Cannot be used at the same time as *color_temperature*.

config_entries.label: Color Correct
config_entries.description: Apply a color correction to each color channel. This defines the maximum brightness of each channel. For example `[100%, 50%, 100%]` would set the green channel to be at most at 50% brightness.

config_entries.label: Color Temperature
config_entries.description: The color temperature (in [mireds](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mired) or Kelvin) of the white channel.

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [this list](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Green
config_entries.description: The green channel of the light.

config_entries.label: Initial State
config_entries.description: The initial state the light should be set to on bootup. This state will be applied when the state is **not** restored based on `restore_mode` (below).

config_entries.label: State
config_entries.description: The ON/OFF state for the light.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: Leading Edge
config_entries.description: [Dimming mode](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dimmer#Solid-state_dimmer): `true` means leading edge, `false` is trailing edge. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Max Brightness
config_entries.description: Maximum brightness value on a scale from 0..1000, the default is 1000.

config_entries.label: Min Brightness
config_entries.description: Minimum brightness value on a scale from 0..1000, the default is 0.

config_entries.label: Power Supply
config_entries.description: The [Power Supply](https://esphome.io/components/power_supply/) to connect to this light. When the light is turned on, the power supply will automatically be switched on too.

config_entries.label: Red
config_entries.description: The red channel of the light.

config_entries.label: Warm White
config_entries.description: The brightness of the warm white channel. Cannot be used at the same time as *color_temperature*.

config_entries.label: Warmup Brightness
config_entries.description: Brightness threshold below which the dimmer switches on later in mains current cycle. [This might help with dimming LEDs](https://github.com/jamesturton/shelly-dimmer-stm32/pull/23). The value is from 0..1000 with an default of 0.

config_entries.label: White
config_entries.description: The brightness of the white channel.
